description_long: This object is a weathered, derelict airplane fuselage resting on a dark, irregular patch that resembles scorched earth or a barren surface. The fuselage is cylindrical and metallic, with a faded light gray exterior marred by numerous dark spots, corrosion, and visible damage. Several windows line the side, and the cockpit area at the front is partially open or broken, exposing the interior. The wings are truncated or missing, with only short stubs remaining, and the tail section is also incomplete, showing signs of heavy wear and breakage. The surrounding ground appears uneven and rough, further emphasizing the abandoned and deteriorated state of the airplane.
description: A heavily damaged, abandoned airplane fuselage with a corroded gray exterior, missing wings, and a broken cockpit, resting on a rough, dark patch of ground.
description_view_0: The front and left side of the airplane are visible, showing the open cockpit and the short, broken wing stub.
description_view_1: The left side of the fuselage is prominent, with the row of windows and the truncated wing stub clearly visible.
description_view_2: The rear and top of the fuselage are shown, highlighting the missing tail section and the overall weathered surface.
description_view_3: The right side and broken tail area are visible, with the damaged fuselage and open cockpit area in view.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight, truncated wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: No engines visible; nacelles or mounts are absent or damaged.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer, though the tail section is incomplete and heavily damaged.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely missing or retracted due to damage.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, partially open or broken.
